target_game.name: Sand Loop
target_game.core_loop: Tap to release buckets onto a conveyor belt to collect matching colored sand and complete pixel art images.
target_game.audience_proxy: Casual mobile players, likely 25-45, who enjoy satisfying ASMR-style sorting puzzles and creative reveals.
target_game.visual_style: 3D stylized objects with vibrant colors and pixel-art sand compositions.

palette.description: A bright, clean palette dominated by sky blue and white, with high-saturation primary colors for the sand and interactive elements.

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · 3D-render

centroid_hook.summary: Filling a spice jar with red pepper flakes in a clean 3D kitchen.
centroid_hook.visual_action: Red pepper flakes pour into a labeled jar on a pink countertop.

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 143d. Hook representative: "Filling a spice jar with red pepper flakes in a clean 3D kitchen."

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · animation

centroid_hook.summary: Goku Ultra Instinct charging a powerful energy blast.
centroid_hook.visual_action: Goku glows with a blue aura while charging a Kamehameha energy sphere against a rainbow background.

top_archetypes.rationale: 2 creatives across 2 unique advertisers, average age 385d. Hook representative: "Goku Ultra Instinct charging a powerful energy blast."

top_archetypes.label: Transformation · animation

centroid_hook.summary: Goku Super Saiyan 3 unleashes his signature Dragon Fist attack.
centroid_hook.visual_action: Goku charges forward with a glowing fist as a golden dragon aura begins to form.

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 716d. Hook representative: "Goku Super Saiyan 3 unleashes his signature Dragon Fist attack."

top_archetypes.label: Challenge · animation

centroid_hook.summary: High-intensity anime combat featuring Vegito Blue's signature attack.
centroid_hook.visual_action: Vegito Blue charges and fires a massive blue and yellow energy beam.
centroid_hook.voiceover_transcript: Try and survive this! Final Flash!

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 716d. Hook representative: "High-intensity anime combat featuring Vegito Blue's signature attack."

game_fit_scores.rationale: The ASMR-satisfaction emotional pitch and clean 3D-render aesthetic align well with Sand Loop's calm/satisfying tone and casual 25-45 audience, so the audience overlap is real. However, the palette (muted pinks/red) clashes with the game's bright sky-blue/white/multi-saturated identity, and the spice-jar pour hook fundamentally misrepresents the core loop — there's no conveyor belt, no color-sorting decision, no pixel-art reveal payoff. Recommend only as inspiration: borrow the satisfying-pour 3D framing but rebuild around multi-color sand pouring into pixel-art reveals to avoid misleading-ad install churn.

Hard pass. The Goku/Kamehameha anime power-fantasy hook collides with Sand Loop's calm, ASMR sorting DNA on every axis: palette overlaps only superficially (blue), the visual_action shows zero conveyor/sand/pixel-reveal mechanic, and the implied audience (shounen anime fans, often male teens) barely overlaps with 25-45 casual sorting-puzzle players. Add IP/trademark risk on Goku and a stale 385d freshness with only 2 advertisers — not worth testing even as a misdirect hook.

A Dragon Ball-style Super Saiyan transformation hook is fundamentally misaligned with Sand Loop: the high-energy shounen anime aesthetic (fiery oranges, dark blues, screaming voiceover) clashes with the calm, ASMR, pastel sky-blue/white sorting fantasy, and there's no narrative bridge from "power-up punch" to tap-to-sort-sand color matching. The audience overlap is also weak — anime action fans skew younger/male and seek combat catharsis, not satisfying pixel-art reveals. Skip this archetype; if leadership insists on testing "transformation" as an emotional pitch, reframe it as a sand-pile-to-pixel-art reveal transformation rather than borrowing IP-adjacent action tropes (also IP-risk concern with Goku likeness).

Severe mismatch across the board: high-intensity anime combat with shouted VO and beam attacks contradicts Sand Loop's calm/ASMR sorting fantasy, and the only thread of overlap is incidental palette (blue/yellow). The challenge hook ("Try and survive this!") sets up combat skill expectations the core loop of tapping buckets and color-sorting cannot deliver, guaranteeing post-install churn. Audience skews young male shōnen fans rather than the 25-45 casual satisfying-puzzle demo — do not adapt this archetype; if a "challenge" angle is needed, build a fail-state/near-miss sand-spill version instead.

brief.title: Sand Loop — Pour, Sort, Reveal
brief.hook_3s: Rainbow sand cascades from a tilted bucket onto a glowing conveyor belt — and a pixel-art heart begins to bloom.
scene_flow: 0-3s HOOK: Macro close-up of a 3D bucket tipping; vibrant red sand pours in slow-mo onto a sky-blue conveyor belt, ASMR grain-by-grain.
3-7s SETUP: Camera pulls back to reveal multiple color-coded buckets above the belt and an empty pixel-art frame below waiting to be filled.
7-11s TENSION: A finger taps to release blue and yellow buckets in sequence; sand streams split, lock-and-key obstacle clicks open, one wrong-color stream is shown ALMOST happening (near-miss).
11-14s PAYOFF: Pixel-art image (a smiling sun or heart) completes with a satisfying sparkle and golden confetti burst.
14-15s CTA: Game logo snaps in over the finished pixel art with the download prompt.
brief.visual_direction: Clean 3D-render kitchen-counter aesthetic borrowed from the winning archetype, but rebuilt in Sand Loop's sky-blue (#4A90E2) and crisp white (#FFFFFF) world with golden (#FFD700) highlights on buckets and sparkle FX. Soft studio lighting, shallow depth of field on the pour, and chunky pixel-art sand grains that retain their cubed identity even mid-air. Mood is calm-satisfying ASMR: every grain has weight, every tap has a soft click, every reveal earns a gentle chime.
brief.rationale: Keeps the archetype's proven satisfying-pour 3D framing and ASMR emotional pitch but rebuilds the visuals around Sand Loop's true core loop — color-sorting buckets, conveyor belt, pixel-art reveal — to eliminate misleading-ad install churn flagged in fit analysis. Lead with the highest-arousal 3-second pour hook, then quickly establish the sorting decision so installers arrive expecting (and getting) the actual game. UA should A/B the near-miss beat at 7-11s vs. a clean run to find the higher CTR variant.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, aspect 9:16, macro close-up of a 3D stylized bucket tipping and pouring vibrant pixel-art sand grains onto a glossy sky-blue conveyor belt, an empty pixel-art frame glows below, palette dominated by #4A90E2 sky blue and #FFFFFF clean white with #FFD700 gold accents on the bucket rim and sparkles, soft studio lighting, shallow depth of field, ASMR satisfying mood, on-screen text 'Pour the sand…' in bold rounded white sans-serif, 3D stylized objects with vibrant colors and pixel-art sand compositions.

Storyboard frame, aspect 9:16, top-down three-quarter view of four color-coded 3D buckets (red, blue, yellow, green) suspended above a sky-blue conveyor belt with a lock-and-key obstacle mid-track, streams of cubed pixel-art sand falling in parallel, palette #4A90E2 background, #FFFFFF belt highlights, #FFD700 glowing lock, on-screen text 'Match the colors' in bold white sans-serif, 3D stylized objects with vibrant colors and pixel-art sand compositions.

Storyboard payoff frame, aspect 9:16, completed pixel-art smiling sun made entirely of colored sand grains glowing in the center of the screen with golden confetti bursting outward, sky-blue #4A90E2 backdrop, #FFFFFF rays of light, #FFD700 confetti and sparkle FX, joyful satisfying mood, on-screen text 'Reveal the picture!' in bold rounded white sans-serif, 3D stylized objects with vibrant colors and pixel-art sand compositions.

brief.title: Sand Loop — Charge & Release: The Satisfying Pixel Reveal
brief.hook_3s: Macro shot of jewel-toned sand streams pouring from buckets onto a glowing conveyor — a single grain falls into place with a soft 'tink'.
scene_flow: 0-3s HOOK: Extreme close-up of vibrant sand cascading from a tilting bucket onto a moving conveyor belt; audio = ASMR pour + soft chime.
3-7s SETUP: Pull back to reveal multiple color-coded buckets above a conveyor; finger taps release red sand into a red-coded slot — satisfying snap-to-grid feedback.
7-11s TENSION: A 'wrong color' near-miss — yellow sand drifts toward a blue lane; a perfectly timed tap diverts it. Lock-and-key obstacle clicks open.
11-14s PAYOFF: Camera tilts down to show the pixel-art image (a cute cat) completing grain-by-grain in a sparkling reveal.
14-15s CTA: Finished pixel art glows; logo + 'Play Now' button pulses.
brief.visual_direction: Lean fully into the calm/satisfying ASMR DNA: sky-blue (#4A90E2) backdrop with soft gradient, crisp white (#FFFFFF) conveyor and UI chrome, and gold (#FFD700) accents on buckets, taps, and the final reveal sparkle. Use 3D stylized props (chunky buckets, rounded conveyor) contrasted against crisp pixel-art sand compositions. Macro depth-of-field, shallow focus on sand grains, gentle bloom on the gold accents. Motion is unhurried — every tap produces a clean haptic-style visual pop. No anime tropes, no power-fantasy bombast; the 'satisfaction' beat from the archetype is preserved purely through tactile pour-and-snap moments and the climactic pixel reveal.
brief.rationale: We keep the archetype's 'satisfaction' emotional pitch but swap anime power-charge for ASMR pour-and-reveal — the actual moment-to-moment feel of Sand Loop. The hero beat (macro sand cascade + pixel reveal) is gameplay-truthful, IP-safe, and built to stop the scroll for 25-45 casual puzzlers. UA: test against ASMR/sorting lookalikes; the near-miss beat is the variant lever — A/B with and without the 'Don't mix them up!' tension overlay.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, vertical 9:16 mobile ad still: extreme macro shot of vibrant pixel-art sand grains pouring from a glossy 3D stylized bucket onto a clean white conveyor belt, sky-blue gradient background (#4A90E2), white conveyor (#FFFFFF), gold accents and sparkle highlights (#FFD700), shallow depth of field, soft bloom, calm ASMR mood, 3D stylized objects with vibrant colors and pixel-art sand compositions, on-screen text 'Tap to pour' in bold rounded white sans-serif at top, app store ad composition, ultra crisp, high saturation.

Storyboard frame, vertical 9:16: top-down angled view of three color-coded 3D buckets (red, blue, yellow) hovering above a white conveyor belt carrying streams of matching pixel-art sand into colored slots, sky-blue background (#4A90E2), white belt (#FFFFFF), glowing gold tap indicator (#FFD700) on the red bucket, 3D stylized objects with vibrant colors and pixel-art sand compositions, clean UI, soft shadows, on-screen text 'Match the color' centered in bold white sans-serif, mobile playable ad style.

Storyboard payoff frame, vertical 9:16: a completed pixel-art cat image fully formed from colorful sand grains glowing on the conveyor, gold sparkle particles (#FFD700) bursting upward, sky-blue backdrop (#4A90E2), white frame and UI elements (#FFFFFF), 3D stylized objects with vibrant colors and pixel-art sand compositions, joyful satisfying reveal moment, on-screen text 'Watch it come to life…' in white bold rounded sans-serif at top with a pulsing yellow Play Now button at bottom.

brief.title: Don't Spill The Sand — Challenge Reframe for Sand Loop
brief.hook_3s: Close-up: a single grain of red sand rolls toward the WRONG bucket — "Can you save it in time?"
scene_flow: 0–3s HOOK: Macro ASMR shot of vibrant sand streaming down a conveyor belt; a rogue red grain drifts toward a blue bucket. Text: 'Can you save it?'
3–7s STAKES: Pull back to reveal a half-finished pixel-art heart about to be ruined by one wrong color. A finger hovers, the belt speeds up subtly. Text: '99% fail this level.'
7–11s GAMEPLAY: Tap-tap-tap — buckets swap, locks click open, colored sand cascades into perfect rows. Satisfying pops and soft chimes. Text: 'Tap to sort. Don't spill.'
11–14s PAYOFF: The pixel-art image completes in a sparkle reveal — a glowing heart / sunset / cat. Calm exhale moment. Text: 'Oddly satisfying.'
14–15s CTA: Logo lockup over the finished pixel art with a gentle bounce. Text: 'Play Free'
brief.visual_direction: Lean fully into Sand Loop's calm/ASMR DNA while borrowing only the *structural* tension of the challenge archetype (a near-miss fail state) — never its shouty combat energy. Sky-blue (#4A90E2) backdrop with soft studio lighting, crisp white (#FFFFFF) conveyor rigs, and golden-yellow (#FFD700) accent buckets and sparkles. 3D stylized chunky props with shallow depth of field, macro close-ups on grain-level sand physics, and the signature pixel-art reveal as the emotional climax. Motion is smooth and weighty — no harsh shakes, no flashes; tension comes from a single grain's trajectory, not explosions.
brief.rationale: We keep the archetype's proven challenge/curiosity hook ("Can you survive/save it?") but swap combat stakes for a near-miss sand-spill — preserving CTR drivers without breaking the post-install promise of a calm sorting puzzle. This protects D1 retention for the 25–45 ASMR-puzzle audience while still testing the "challenge" emotional pitch. Recommend A/B testing the 99% fail overlay vs. a pure-ASMR cold open to isolate which hook converts the casual demo best.
